subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze magnetic field patterns and directions using rules critically
Evaluate force on conductors and motor working with quantitative aspects
Apply Fleming's rules in complex scenarios and error analysis in experiments
Understand solenoid as electromagnet with factors quantitatively
Critically assess domestic circuit safety and faults with real-world reasoning
Develop experimental, analytical, and problem-solving skills

lesson_plan.title: Magnetic Field Due to Conductors – Advanced Patterns
topics_covered: Magnetic field lines properties and conventions
Field due to straight conductor and circular loop (centre formula qualitative)
teaching_strategy: Advanced demonstration with iron filings for loop and straight wire
Derive direction using rule in multiple orientations
student_activities: Plot field lines using compass for straight wire and loop at multiple points
Solve direction prediction problems with diagrams
assessment: Field line patterns and direction accuracy

lesson_plan.title: Solenoid and Electromagnet – Quantitative Analysis
topics_covered: Factors affecting field strength (current, turns, length, core)
teaching_strategy: Experiment to vary current/turns and observe attraction
Graphical discussion on field vs factors
student_activities: Vary parameters in solenoid setup, record paper clip count (force proxy)
Plot graph of strength vs turns/current

lesson_plan.title: Force on Conductor and Fleming's Rules
topics_covered: Force magnitude and direction (F = BIL sinθ qualitative)
student_activities: Predict force direction in 8-10 varied scenarios (angle, reversal)
Perform force demo and note errors (alignment, current stability)

lesson_plan.title: Electric Motor – Construction and Working Analysis
teaching_strategy: Dissect motor model, explain each part
Discussion on back EMF and practical efficiency
student_activities: Build/analyze motor model, reverse current to observe direction change
Solve advanced questions on commutator function

lesson_plan.title: Domestic Circuits – Safety and Fault Analysis
topics_covered: Series vs parallel in household wiring
Role of fuse, MCB, earthing in detail
teaching_strategy: Model of complete domestic circuit with faults simulation
student_activities: Draw detailed circuit with calculations for current distribution
Analyze fault scenarios (why fuse blows, earthing saves)
Propose safety improvements for given wiring diagram

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Advanced Problems, and Critical Assessment
teaching_strategy: Hard-level quiz with diagrams and applications
student_activities: Solve 15-20 hard questions (including graphs, directions, faults)
Reflect on chapter with real-world critique

teaching_aids: Copper wire, battery, compass, iron filings, horse-shoe magnet
DC motor kit, split ring model
Graph paper, whiteboard for rules and diagrams
teacher_tip: Stress consistent use of right/left-hand rules with physical gestures. Incorporate error analysis in every experiment to build scientific temperament. Challenge students with 'why' and 'what if' questions to deepen understanding beyond rote application.
